tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I thought it might help if I shared how I like to study. I'm a first-year law student and I tend to get overwhelmed when I have to read dense material all at once. I learn best when I break things down into smaller chunks and take lots of breaks. Could you remember this about me for our future study sessions?

function_call.arguments: { "text": "User is a first-year law student who gets overwhelmed with dense material. They learn best by breaking things into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks. This should guide how study sessions are structured."}

messages.content: I'll remember that for you! As a first-year law student, you prefer breaking dense material into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks to avoid feeling overwhelmed. I'll keep this in mind for our future study sessions together.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User is a first-year law student who gets overwhelmed with dense material. They learn best by breaking things into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks. This should guide how study sessions are structured."}

They learn best by breaking things into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks. This should guide how study sessions are structured."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Got it! I've saved your study preferences. For future sessions, I'll make sure we break down dense material into smaller chunks and build in plenty of breaks to keep things manageable for you. Good luck with your first year of law school! 🎓
Hey, just curious - can you show me what you saved about my study preferences? I want to make sure it's all there for our future sessions.

They learn best by breaking things into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks. This should guide how study sessions are structured.", "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what I saved for you:

**Study Preferences:**
- First-year law student
- Gets overwhelmed with dense material
- Learns best by breaking things into smaller chunks
- Benefits from taking lots of breaks during study sessions

This will guide how I help structure our future study sessions. Looks like it's all there! Let me know if you'd like me to add anything else.

Oh that's great, it all looks correct! But wait, is there anything else you've saved about me, or is that the only thing in storage? I just want to make sure I know what information you have for our future sessions.

That was the only memory I had stored for you, and now I've added your study preferences. So currently, that's the only information I have saved about you for our future sessions.

If there's anything else you'd like me to remember—like your favorite subjects, preferred times to study, or any other learning preferences—just let me know!

Hey, that's good to know there's only the one memory saved. Since we're keeping things clean and relevant, could you double-check if there's anything in storage that might be outdated or incorrect that we should remove? I just want to make sure we're starting with accurate information for our future study sessions.

I just checked, and the only memory in storage is the one we just added about your study preferences:

**"User is a first-year law student who gets overwhelmed with dense material. They learn best by breaking things into smaller chunks and taking lots of breaks. This should guide how study sessions are structured."**

That came directly from what you told me, so it's accurate and relevant. There are no outdated or incorrect memories to remove—storage is clean! 

Let me know if anything changes or if you'd like to add more details.
That makes sense - since the only memory is what I just told you and it's accurate, there's nothing to remove. Thanks for checking! I think we're all set for our study sessions now.

You're welcome! You're all set—just the one accurate memory about your study preferences. Looking forward to helping you with your future law school study sessions whenever you're ready. Good luck with your studies! 📚
